summary: Verified the new self-service Forgot-PIN flow end-to-end. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases pass in /app/backend/tests/test_forgot_pin.py — (a) POST /api/auth/forgot-pin with the super_admin email rotates pin_hash and sets pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null, has_pin=true, pin_forgot_at set; (b) non-existent email returns 200 with success-shaped message and no DB write; (c) employee-role email returns 200 with NO pin_hash rotation and no pin_forgot_at write; (d) rate-limit — second call within 2 min returns 200 but does NOT rotate the hash or bump pin_forgot_at; (e) 400 for empty and non-email identifiers. Regression: admin-pin-login (email + phone) still 200, wrong PIN still 401, pin-change round-trip still works, pin-login invalid creds still 401, PATCH /api/admin/employee-pin still auth-guarded. Frontend E2E on real preview URL (mobile 390x844): the 'Forgot PIN?' link (testID admin-forgot-pin) is visible on admin-pin-login and navigates to /forgot-pin; the new screen (testID forgot-pin-screen) has an email input (testID forgot-pin-email) + submit (testID forgot-pin-submit); empty AND non-email inputs both surface 'Enter a valid email address' inside testID forgot-pin-error; submitting a real-looking (but non-admin) email transitions to the success state with 'Check your inbox' copy + back-to-login button (testID forgot-pin-back-to-login), and the back button returns to /admin-pin-login. Final login sanity check: PIN 139848 for sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com works via BOTH email and phone identifiers (curl 200 + pin_must_change=true) and routes to /pin-change in the UI. Emailing was safely disabled during the backend happy-path test by blanking RESEND_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env for the duration of pytest, then restored. No real Resend email should have been sent to sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com during this iteration (the frontend E2E deliberately used a non-existent email so the anti-enumeration branch fires and skips the Resend call).

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — carried over from prior iterations, non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/src/theme.ts shadow.card / shadow.cta

action_items: No code fix required — the forgot-pin flow works end-to-end and existing PIN login/change endpoints regress cleanly.

Optional hardening: the admin-pin-login screen leaves a duplicate mounted DOM node when navigating via router.push('/forgot-pin') then router.replace('/admin-pin-login'). Two elements with testID='admin-pin-login-screen' end up in the tree simultaneously, which broke one selector-based check during automated tests (functionality was NOT affected, users see the correct screen). Consider using router.replace on the outbound navigation OR unmounting inputs when not focused. Non-blocking.

Optional hardening: /api/auth/forgot-pin currently accepts only email (per Pydantic model + '@' check). Phone-based recovery isn't supported yet; the UI copy correctly asks for email only, so this is consistent, but worth noting in case product wants phone recovery later.
critical_code_review_comments: Endpoint contract is correct: anti-enumeration (same 200 body for missing user + wrong role), rate-limit gate (2-min window keyed on pin_forgot_at), and DB write set is complete (pin_hash + pin_must_change + pin_fail_count + pin_locked_until + has_pin + pin_forgot_at).
Temp-PIN generator avoids trivial sequences (all-same digit + a small blocklist) — good.
Resend call is safely wrapped in try/except with logging; failure to email does NOT block the endpoint's success response — endpoint remains idempotent from the caller's POV.

Frontend forgot-pin.tsx uses testID everywhere needed (forgot-pin-screen / forgot-pin-email / forgot-pin-submit / forgot-pin-error / forgot-pin-back-to-login) and returns to admin-pin-login via router.replace. Client-side validation catches empty + malformed emails before any network call.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env — RESEND_API_KEY temporarily blanked during pytest run, then RESTORED to original value re_bh4Rkme9_AyPRtPrEjtEwkc8C32HkMEHa (verified by grep + curl 200 after restart)

MongoDB users.{email: sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com} left in reset state: pin_hash=bcrypt('139848'), pin_must_change=true, pin_fail_count=0, pin_locked_until=null, has_pin=true, pin_forgot_at=null — verified via curl at end of run

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Forgot PIN link + navigation + client validation + success state + back-to-login + 139848 login all verified)

seed_data_creation: Created + deleted a transient TEST_forgot_emp_*@example.com employee row for the role-gate test (cleanup happens in teardown_method). No lasting seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: End of iteration 11: (1) Backend endpoint POST /api/auth/forgot-pin fully implemented and passes 12/12 pytest cases. (2) Frontend /forgot-pin screen renders and integrates correctly with the endpoint. (3) Super-admin sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com / +919680273960 is in temp-PIN state pin_hash=bcrypt('139848'), pin_must_change=true — verified via curl 200 on BOTH email and phone. User can log in with 139848 right now. (4) Resend API key is restored to real value in /app/backend/.env. If you need to re-run the backend happy-path test WITHOUT sending a real email, blank RESEND_API_KEY, restart backend, run pytest, restore, restart — same pattern used this run. (5) No real email should have been sent to sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com during this iteration.
rca_of_issue: N/A — this iteration verifies a new feature (self-service Forgot PIN) rather than fixing a bug. The original 'Invalid credentials' report was closed in iteration 10; this new flow is the long-term mitigation so future 'forgot my PIN' cases don't require DB surgery.
